rows.description: Computus Dni Rectoris J L Richards
rows.notes: Topmost title line. Handwriting is legible but abbreviations and some letters (Dni, initials) are slightly stylized; no monetary amounts present. Column boundaries determined from row 27 as instructed.
Date line underneath title. Script clear though slightly slanted; no amounts. Used page tilt guidance for vertical alignment.
Third top line is readable but some letters are ambiguous (final word uncertain). No monetary values; alignment reference from final total (row 27).
Centered section heading. Large, clear script; no amounts. Column boundaries set from row 27.
Sub-heading under main heading. Script legible; no monetary amounts on this line.
Clear entry and amount '60' aligned to pounds column (using row 27 boundaries). No shillings/pence visible. Numbers and text are unambiguous.
Amount '10' clearly in pounds column. Description handwriting legible; 'Do' indicates same as above.
Amount reads clearly as '1 7 6' (pounds, shillings, pence) using the row 27 column alignment. Description is partially ambiguous (letters in names uncertain), so confidence reduced for the text.
Monetary columns line up to '9 6 8' (9 pounds, 6 shillings, 8 pence) when projected from row 27. Description mostly legible though 'Lad' could be 'Lad(e)'.
Amount '6 13 4' is clear in the three right-hand columns. The description line is abbreviated and some letters hard to interpret ('15. midm' uncertain), reducing confidence.
Numbers read as '1 13 4' using the column boundaries from row 27. The name looks like 'Pickford' but final characters (the '13?') are not perfectly clear in the script.
Amount '16 13 4' aligns well to pounds/shillings/pence columns. The place-name (transcribed as 'Medesham') is moderately uncertain due to cursive style.
Amount '1 6 1' is readable and within valid ranges. Description mostly legible; 'Colman' and 'Bucks' inferred from script but clear enough.
Amount interpreted as '17 6' (17 pounds, 6 shillings) with no pence visible. Name 'Lymath' read from script though could be a variant spelling; monetary columns validated against row 27.
This line appears crossed out/struck-through and has no visible aligned amounts. Text is heavily obscured by the crossing, making reading uncertain; treated as an entry with no amount.
Clear centered section heading in large script. No amounts on the header line. Column boundaries anchored to row 27.
Amount reads as '11 2 9' when aligned with the established column boundaries. Handwriting for the place and period notation is legible.
Clear amount '28' in the pounds column. Description 'Long Wittenham Rectory' clearly legible.
Amount '14' clear in pounds column. Description 'Garston Rectory' readable; no shillings/pence indicated on this line.
Section heading centered on the page and clearly written. No monetary values on this line.
Amount interpretable as '91 2 6' using the row 27 column alignment. The word 'Augmentation' is clear; a small numeral/abbreviation next to it is faint, giving slight uncertainty in the description.
Amount reads '102 7 5' clearly in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Description mostly legible, minor ambiguity in the year indicator from the script.

Pounds/shillings parse as '17 11' and pence column shows '4' with a small half-penny loop (interpreted as 1/2) — hence 4½ d. The place name is moderately uncertain in spelling, so moderate confidence.
Amount '7 10 2' aligns with column boundaries from row 27. 'Do' indicates same parish/entry; the rest of the description is legible.
Amount '238 15 10' is clearly written and validated against the column alignment. The note about tax deducted is legible; the minor uncertainty is in a small numeral/abbrev after '15'.
Amount '44 9 4' clearly in the three monetary columns. Description readable; validated by column projection from row 27.

Final total used as the definitive alignment reference per page-specific guidance. Clearly reads '806 17 1/2' (806 pounds, 17 shillings, 1½ pence). Column boundaries were projected upward from this line.
